title: Legal frameworks addressing violence against women
subtitle: A higher percentage indicates stronger legislation.
caption: Across countries in 2024, the percentage of legal frameworks addressing violence against women varies widely, with darker shades of blue representing stronger legislation. The map shows the highest values, falling in the 80% to 100% band, across North America, most of South America, Western and Central Europe, Australia, and parts of East Asia including China and Japan. In contrast, the lowest values appear across much of sub-Saharan Africa and parts of the Middle East, where many countries fall in the 0% to 30% bands or are marked as having no data. A middle range of legislation, roughly 40% to 70%, is visible in countries such as India, Russia, and several nations in Southeast Asia and Southern Africa. The geographic distribution shows a concentration of comprehensive legal frameworks in the Americas and Europe, while significant gaps remain in Africa and parts of Asia.
